title: leptoderris fasciculata var. letestui
intro: Leptoderris fasciculata var. letestui is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of central Africa, particularly in the Republic of Congo. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of rotenone and other rotenoids, which exhibit insecticidal, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Leptoderris fasciculata var. letestui include its potential to treat various diseases, such as malaria, fever, and rheumatism, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the leaves and stems, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea or decoction for medicinal purposes.
